en_definition: to consider something when making a decision or judgment
example1: The grade takes into account both the essay and the presentation.
example1_ru: Оценка учитывает как эссе, так и презентацию.
example2: We must take into account the extra costs before deciding.

en_definition: to decide something is true after considering the facts
example1: It is too early to draw a conclusion from just one trial.
example1_ru: Ещё слишком рано делать вывод на основе всего одного испытания.
example2: The report draws a clear conclusion from the survey data.

en_definition: considering everything; generally speaking
example1: On the whole, the experiment supported our original hypothesis.
example1_ru: В целом эксперимент подтвердил нашу первоначальную гипотезу.
example2: On the whole, students found the new textbook helpful.

en_definition: used to introduce a specific example
example1: Some elements, for instance sodium, react violently with water.
example1_ru: Некоторые элементы, например натрий, бурно реагируют с водой.
example2: For instance, the second experiment gave completely different results.

en_definition: used to introduce a brief summary of what has been said
example1: To sum up, the experiment confirmed the initial prediction.
example1_ru: Подводя итог, эксперимент подтвердил первоначальный прогноз.
example2: To sum up, three main factors affect the outcome.

en_definition: used to give a short statement of the main points
example1: In summary, the report recommends three changes to the policy.
example1_ru: Резюмируя, отчёт рекомендует три изменения в политике.
example2: In summary, the findings support the original theory.

en_definition: to remember and consider a fact when doing something
example1: Bear in mind that the library closes early on Fridays.
example1_ru: Имейте в виду, что библиотека по пятницам закрывается рано.
example2: You should bear in mind the limitations of this method.

en_definition: used to introduce the effect of something just mentioned
example1: The lab was closed; as a result, the experiment was delayed.
example1_ru: Лаборатория была закрыта, в результате эксперимент отложили.
example2: She studied hard and, as a result, passed with distinction.

en_definition: caused by; because of
example1: The class was cancelled due to a scheduling conflict.
example1_ru: Занятие отменили из-за накладки в расписании.
example2: Due to heavy rain, the field trip was postponed.

en_definition: on condition that; provided that
example1: You may retake the test as long as you register in advance.
example1_ru: Вы можете пересдать тест при условии, что зарегистрируетесь заранее.
example2: As long as the data is accurate, the model should work.

en_definition: despite the fact that
example1: Even though the experiment failed, the team learned a lot.
example1_ru: Хотя эксперимент не удался, команда многому научилась.
example2: She kept working even though she was exhausted.

en_definition: with the purpose of achieving something
example1: In order to pass, you must attend at least eighty percent of classes.
example1_ru: Чтобы сдать курс, вы должны посещать не менее восьмидесяти процентов занятий.
example2: The scientists repeated the test in order to confirm the result.

ru_translation: с тем чтобы, для того чтобы
en_definition: in order to; with the purpose that
example1: Data was anonymized so as to protect participants' privacy.
example1_ru: Данные были обезличены, чтобы защитить конфиденциальность участников.
example2: Arrive early so as to avoid the rush at registration.

ru_translation: в той мере, в какой; постольку поскольку
en_definition: to the extent that
example1: The theory is useful insofar as it predicts future behavior.
example1_ru: Теория полезна в той мере, в какой она предсказывает будущее поведение.
example2: The plan is sound insofar as the assumptions hold true.

en_definition: in order to achieve the aim just mentioned
example1: We want to reduce errors; to that end, we added a review step.
example1_ru: Мы хотим сократить количество ошибок; с этой целью мы добавили этап проверки.
example2: The university plans to increase funding; to that end, it launched a campaign.

en_definition: used to give more exact detail about what has just been mentioned
example1: Two factors matter most, namely time and cost.
example1_ru: Важнее всего два фактора, а именно время и стоимость.
example2: One country, namely Japan, was excluded from the study.

en_definition: used to explain more clearly what was just said
example1: The results were mixed, that is to say, neither clearly positive nor negative.
example1_ru: Результаты были неоднозначными, то есть ни явно положительными, ни отрицательными.
example2: He is a postgraduate, that is to say, he already has a bachelor's degree.

en_definition: used to express something in an easier, clearer way
example1: Put simply, the experiment measures how fast the gas expands.
example1_ru: Проще говоря, эксперимент измеряет скорость расширения газа.
example2: Put simply, the theory did not match the observed data.

headword: as far as x is concerned
en_definition: used to introduce a topic being discussed specifically
example1: As far as grading is concerned, both essays receive equal weight.
example1_ru: Что касается выставления оценок, оба эссе имеют одинаковый вес.
example2: As far as safety is concerned, the new lab meets all standards.

en_definition: used to emphasize that a second fact is true in addition to the first
example1: The course covers not only theory but also practical skills.
example1_ru: Курс охватывает не только теорию, но и практические навыки.
example2: She is not only intelligent but also very hardworking.

en_definition: to ask for information in a formal or polite way
example1: I am writing to enquire about the status of my application.
example1_ru: Я пишу, чтобы узнать о статусе моего заявления.
example2: Please enquire at the front desk about lost property.

en_definition: used in polite formal requests to mean please
example1: Kindly find attached the requested documents for your records.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, найдите во вложении запрошенные документы для ваших записей.
example2: Could you kindly confirm your attendance by Friday?

en_definition: included with this letter or email
example1: Please find attached herewith a copy of your official transcript.
example1_ru: К настоящему письму прилагается копия вашей официальной выписки.
example2: Attached herewith is the signed agreement for your records.

ru_translation: при первой возможности, как можно скорее
en_definition: as soon as possible, in a polite formal way
example1: Please reply at your earliest convenience regarding the meeting time.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, ответьте при первой возможности насчёт времени встречи.
example2: We would appreciate your response at your earliest convenience.

en_definition: a formal conditional way to say if you need
example1: Should you require further information, please contact the office.
example1_ru: Если вам потребуется дополнительная информация, пожалуйста, свяжитесь с офисом.
example2: Should you require an extension, submit your request in writing.

en_definition: a formal way to introduce bad news
example1: I regret to inform you that your application was unsuccessful.
example1_ru: С сожалением сообщаю вам, что ваша заявка не была одобрена.
example2: I regret to inform you that the event has been cancelled.

en_definition: a formal phrase used to inform someone of important information
example1: Please be advised that the office will be closed next Monday.
example1_ru: Обращаем ваше внимание, что офис будет закрыт в следующий понедельник.
example2: Please be advised that late submissions will not be accepted.

en_definition: at the appropriate time in the future
example1: Your certificate will be issued in due course.
example1_ru: Ваш сертификат будет выдан в своё время.
example2: We will contact you in due course with a decision.

en_definition: letters or emails exchanged between people
example1: Please keep a copy of all correspondence with the admissions office.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, сохраняйте копии всей переписки с приёмной комиссией.
example2: The correspondence between the two departments took several weeks.

en_definition: to confirm that something has been received
example1: Please acknowledge receipt of this email by replying.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, подтвердите получение этого письма ответом.
example2: The office will acknowledge receipt of your application within two days.

en_definition: an amount of money still owed
example1: You cannot register until the outstanding balance is paid.
example1_ru: Вы не сможете зарегистрироваться, пока не погасите задолженность.
example2: Please clear your outstanding balance before the end of term.

en_definition: papers submitted to prove the facts stated in a form
example1: Submit all supporting documents together with the application form.
example1_ru: Подайте все подтверждающие документы вместе с формой заявления.
example2: Missing supporting documents will delay your visa application.

en_definition: an official document showing where a person lives
example1: A utility bill can serve as proof of address.
example1_ru: Счёт за коммунальные услуги может служить подтверждением адреса.
example2: You will need proof of address to open a bank account.

en_definition: a person's closest living relative
example1: List your next of kin on the emergency contact form.
example1_ru: Укажите вашего ближайшего родственника в форме экстренного контакта.
example2: The hospital contacted her next of kin immediately.

en_definition: to formally ask for a decision to be reconsidered
example1: Students may appeal a decision within ten working days.
example1_ru: Студенты могут обжаловать решение в течение десяти рабочих дней.
example2: He decided to appeal the exam board's decision.

en_definition: facts that provide a reasonable excuse for poor performance
example1: Illness can be submitted as mitigating circumstances for a missed exam.
example1_ru: Болезнь можно указать как смягчающее обстоятельство при пропуске экзамена.
example2: The panel accepted her mitigating circumstances and granted an extension.

en_definition: to officially decide that a charge does not need to be paid
example1: The university waived the late fee due to a system error.
example1_ru: Университет отменил штраф за опоздание из-за сбоя в системе.
example2: Scholarship students often have their application fee waived.

en_definition: the state of having the right qualities to be allowed to do something
example1: Check your eligibility before applying for the scholarship.
example1_ru: Проверьте своё право на участие перед подачей заявки на стипендию.
example2: Eligibility depends on your grade point average and attendance.

en_definition: the act of obeying a rule, law, or standard
example1: The lab was inspected for compliance with safety regulations.
example1_ru: Лабораторию проверили на соблюдение норм безопасности.
example2: Compliance with the dress code is mandatory during the exam.

en_definition: an official punishment for breaking rules
example1: Cheating on an exam can result in disciplinary action.
example1_ru: Списывание на экзамене может привести к дисциплинарному взысканию.
example2: The committee decided not to take disciplinary action this time.

en_definition: to carry out a scientific test to discover something
example1: Students conducted an experiment to measure reaction time.
example1_ru: Студенты провели эксперимент для измерения времени реакции.
example2: The team plans to conduct further experiments next semester.

en_definition: to write down or store observations from an experiment
example1: Record the data in a table after every measurement.
example1_ru: Фиксируйте данные в таблице после каждого измерения.
example2: Make sure to record data accurately during the trial.

en_definition: to draw the results of an experiment on a graph
example1: Plot the results on a graph to see the trend clearly.
example1_ru: Постройте график результатов, чтобы наглядно увидеть тенденцию.
example2: We plotted the results and noticed a clear pattern.

en_definition: to run an experiment again to check the results
example1: We repeated the trial three times to ensure accuracy.
example1_ru: Мы повторили испытание три раза, чтобы обеспечить точность.
example2: The professor asked us to repeat the trial with a larger sample.

en_definition: a consistent error caused by a flaw in the method or equipment
example1: A systematic error in the scale affected every measurement.
example1_ru: Систематическая ошибка в шкале повлияла на все измерения.
example2: Calibrating the instrument reduces systematic error.

en_definition: an unpredictable error that varies between measurements
example1: Random error can be reduced by taking more measurements.
example1_ru: Случайную ошибку можно уменьшить, увеличив число измерений.
example2: The difference between the two readings was due to random error.

en_definition: to remove a factor from an experiment so it does not affect the result
example1: The design eliminates the variable of temperature by using a constant setting.
example1_ru: Дизайн исключает переменную температуры, используя постоянные настройки.
example2: We eliminated humidity as a variable to simplify the model.

en_definition: to produce a particular outcome or finding
example1: The new method yielded more accurate results than before.
example1_ru: Новый метод дал более точные результаты, чем раньше.
example2: Both tests yielded a similar conclusion.

en_definition: to explain the cause of something, or to make up a certain amount
example1: Human error may account for the difference between the two results.
example1_ru: Человеческий фактор может объяснять разницу между двумя результатами.
example2: Tuition fees account for most of the university's income.

en_definition: to cause something to happen or exist
example1: The policy change gave rise to widespread debate on campus.
example1_ru: Изменение политики породило широкие споры в кампусе.
example2: Small errors can give rise to large problems later.

en_definition: to originate or develop as a result of something
example1: The delay stemmed from a shortage of raw materials.
example1_ru: Задержка произошла из-за нехватки сырья.
example2: Her interest in chemistry stems from a high school project.

en_definition: to depend completely on a particular factor
example1: The success of the project hinges on securing enough funding.
example1_ru: Успех проекта полностью зависит от получения достаточного финансирования.
example2: Whether we finish on time hinges on the weather.

en_definition: to be the basic or most important point of a situation
example1: The whole debate boils down to a question of cost.
example1_ru: Весь спор сводится к вопросу стоимости.
example2: It all boils down to how much time you invest.

en_definition: to add up to a particular total or to be equivalent to something
example1: The total expenses amount to more than the original budget.
example1_ru: Общие расходы составляют больше, чем изначальный бюджет.
example2: His comment amounted to an admission of the mistake.

en_definition: to help explain or make something clearer
example1: The new study sheds light on how memory works.
example1_ru: Новое исследование проливает свет на то, как работает память.
example2: These records shed light on the university's early history.

en_definition: to make people less certain that something is true
example1: The new evidence casts doubt on the original conclusion.
example1_ru: Новые доказательства ставят под сомнение первоначальный вывод.
example2: Errors in the data cast doubt on the whole study.

en_definition: to provide evidence that supports an idea
example1: These findings lend support to the earlier hypothesis.
example1_ru: Эти результаты подкрепляют более раннюю гипотезу.
example2: The survey results lend support to the proposed changes.

en_definition: to be the opposite of or in conflict with something
example1: This finding runs counter to previous research on the topic.
example1_ru: Этот результат противоречит предыдущим исследованиям по теме.
example2: Her proposal runs counter to university policy.

en_definition: to begin to have an effect on a situation
example1: Several other factors come into play when calculating the final grade.
example1_ru: При расчёте итоговой оценки вступают в игру несколько других факторов.
example2: Once demand rises, price differences come into play.

en_definition: to cause doubt about whether something is true or correct
example1: The scandal called into question the reliability of the entire report.
example1_ru: Скандал поставил под вопрос надёжность всего отчёта.
example2: New data called into question the safety of the product.

en_definition: showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise
example1: The scientist kept meticulous records of every measurement.
example1_ru: Учёный вёл тщательные записи каждого измерения.
example2: Meticulous planning made the project run smoothly.

en_definition: done with great care and thoroughness
example1: The translation required painstaking attention to every word.
example1_ru: Перевод требовал кропотливого внимания к каждому слову.
example2: It took painstaking research to trace the original source.

en_definition: very strict and demanding, especially about rules or standards
example1: The laboratory follows stringent safety procedures at all times.
example1_ru: Лаборатория всегда следует строгим процедурам безопасности.
example2: Stringent grading criteria make the course quite challenging.

en_definition: showing care and effort in one's work or duties
example1: A diligent student reviews notes after every lecture.
example1_ru: Прилежный студент просматривает конспекты после каждой лекции.
example2: Her diligent research led to an impressive final paper.

en_definition: done completely and carefully, missing nothing
example1: The reviewer gave the paper a thorough reading before commenting.
example1_ru: Рецензент внимательно прочитал статью перед тем, как оставить комментарии.
example2: A thorough investigation revealed the true cause of the failure.

en_definition: very small in amount and less than what is needed
example1: The team had meagre resources to complete the project.
example1_ru: У команды были скудные ресурсы для завершения проекта.
example2: Their meagre budget limited the scope of the study.

en_definition: existing in large amounts; more than enough
example1: The library has an abundant collection of scientific journals.
example1_ru: В библиотеке имеется обширная коллекция научных журналов.
example2: This region has an abundant supply of fresh water.

en_definition: not enough for the demand; hard to find
example1: Funding for pure research is becoming increasingly scarce.
example1_ru: Финансирование фундаментальных исследований становится всё более скудным.
example2: Clean drinking water is scarce in some regions.

en_definition: in a very careful and precise way
example1: She meticulously checked every calculation before submitting her final report.
example1_ru: Она тщательно проверила каждый расчёт перед сдачей итогового отчёта.
example2: The lab equipment was meticulously cleaned before the experiment.

en_definition: in a way that always happens or behaves the same
example1: The results were consistently accurate across all trials.
example1_ru: Результаты были стабильно точными во всех испытаниях.
example2: She consistently submits assignments before the deadline.

en_definition: always, without exception
example1: Late assignments are invariably penalized by ten percent.
example1_ru: Работы, сданные с опозданием, неизменно штрафуются на десять процентов.
example2: He invariably arrives ten minutes early for lectures.

en_definition: in a way that is clearly noticeable
example1: Scores improved markedly after the new teaching method was introduced.
example1_ru: После введения нового метода обучения результаты заметно улучшились.
example2: The two samples differ markedly in composition.

en_definition: in a way worth noting; especially
example1: Several factors, notably cost, influenced the final decision.
example1_ru: На итоговое решение повлияли несколько факторов, в частности стоимость.
example2: The results were notably better in the second group.

en_definition: approximately; not exactly
example1: The experiment took roughly three hours to complete.
example1_ru: Эксперимент занял приблизительно три часа.
example2: Roughly half of the students chose the optional module.

en_definition: in an exact and accurate way
example1: Measure the liquid precisely before adding it to the mixture.
example1_ru: Точно отмерьте жидкость, прежде чем добавить её в смесь.
example2: That is precisely the problem we need to solve.

en_definition: only; not involving anything or anyone else
example1: The grant is solely for undergraduate research projects.
example1_ru: Грант предназначен исключительно для студенческих исследовательских проектов.
example2: The decision rests solely with the department head.

en_definition: mostly or mainly
example1: The class is predominantly made up of first-year students.
example1_ru: Класс состоит преимущественно из студентов первого курса.
example2: The region is predominantly agricultural.

ru_translation: всё более, всё в большей степени
en_definition: more and more as time passes
example1: Online courses are becoming increasingly popular among students.
example1_ru: Онлайн-курсы становятся всё более популярными среди студентов.
example2: The task became increasingly difficult as deadlines approached.

en_definition: in an organized, methodical way
example1: The team systematically reviewed every data point for errors.
example1_ru: Команда систематически проверила каждую точку данных на наличие ошибок.
example2: Samples were systematically labeled and stored.

en_definition: to work together with others on a shared task
example1: Students are encouraged to collaborate on the group assignment.
example1_ru: Студентов поощряют сотрудничать при выполнении группового задания.
example2: Two departments collaborated on the new research project.

en_definition: the process of working together to produce something
example1: The project is a collaboration between three universities.
example1_ru: Проект — это сотрудничество трёх университетов.
example2: Successful collaboration requires clear communication.

en_definition: a person who helps a group discussion or activity run smoothly
example1: The seminar facilitator kept the discussion on track.
example1_ru: Ведущий семинара удерживал дискуссию в нужном русле.
example2: As facilitator, she encouraged quieter students to speak.

en_definition: a person who controls a discussion or debate
example1: The moderator asked each speaker to keep to five minutes.
example1_ru: Модератор попросил каждого выступающего уложиться в пять минут.
example2: A good moderator keeps the discussion fair and balanced.

en_definition: the main speaker at a conference or event
example1: The keynote speaker discussed recent breakthroughs in genetics.
example1_ru: Основной докладчик рассказал о последних прорывах в генетике.
example2: Students had a chance to ask the keynote speaker questions.

en_definition: a discussion involving several experts in front of an audience
example1: The panel discussion covered the future of renewable energy.
example1_ru: Панельная дискуссия была посвящена будущему возобновляемой энергетики.
example2: Four professors took part in the panel discussion.

en_definition: a person who leads a practical training session
example1: The workshop facilitator demonstrated each step before we tried it.
example1_ru: Ведущий мастер-класса продемонстрировал каждый шаг, прежде чем мы его повторили.
example2: Ask the workshop facilitator if you need extra help.

en_definition: a detailed study of a specific person, event, or situation
example1: The lecture used a case study to explain the theory.
example1_ru: На лекции использовалось тематическое исследование для объяснения теории.
example2: Students analyze a different case study every week.

en_definition: a visit to a place outside the classroom for study purposes
example1: The geology class went on a field trip to the coast.
example1_ru: Группа по геологии отправилась на выездное занятие на побережье.
example2: Bring a notebook for notes during the field trip.

en_definition: a visit made to see or inspect a particular location
example1: Engineering students take a site visit to a construction project.
example1_ru: Студенты-инженеры совершают выезд на строительный объект.
example2: The site visit helped us understand the design in practice.

en_definition: a test that assesses hands-on skills rather than theory
example1: The practical exam requires you to set up the apparatus correctly.
example1_ru: На практическом экзамене нужно правильно собрать оборудование.
example2: She passed the theory exam but failed the practical exam.

en_definition: an oral examination, especially for a research degree
example1: She defended her thesis in a two-hour viva.
example1_ru: Она защищала свою диссертацию на двухчасовом устном экзамене.
example2: The viva panel asked probing questions about the methodology.

en_definition: an exam in which students may use notes and textbooks
example1: The open-book exam still requires strong understanding of the topic.
example1_ru: Экзамен с использованием материалов всё равно требует глубокого понимания темы.
example2: Bring your notes since this is an open-book exam.

en_definition: an exam that students complete outside the classroom within a set period
example1: The take-home exam is due within forty-eight hours.
example1_ru: Экзамен на дому нужно сдать в течение сорока восьми часов.
example2: Take-home exams still require you to work independently.

en_definition: a grade that is just barely enough to pass
example1: His score was a borderline pass, so the exam was reviewed again.
example1_ru: Его балл был на грани зачёта, поэтому работу пересмотрели ещё раз.
example2: Borderline passes are usually double-checked by a second examiner.

en_definition: the score that separates one grade from another
example1: The grade boundary for a distinction is seventy percent.
example1_ru: Граница для оценки «отлично» составляет семьдесят процентов.
example2: She missed the grade boundary by just one point.

en_definition: the process of checking that grading is fair and consistent
example1: All essays go through moderation before final grades are released.
example1_ru: Все эссе проходят проверку объективности перед выставлением итоговых оценок.
example2: Moderation ensures that different examiners grade consistently.

en_definition: a second examiner who independently checks a piece of work
example1: A second marker reviewed the borderline dissertation to confirm the grade.
example1_ru: Второй проверяющий пересмотрел спорную диссертацию, чтобы подтвердить оценку.
example2: If grades differ, the second marker discusses it with the first.

en_definition: an examiner from another institution who checks standards
example1: The external examiner confirmed that the grading was fair.
example1_ru: Внешний экзаменатор подтвердил, что оценивание было справедливым.
example2: Every dissertation is reviewed by an external examiner.

en_definition: an official record of a student's grades and courses
example1: Order your academic transcript from the registrar's office.
example1_ru: Закажите академическую справку в деканате.
example2: The visa application requires a certified academic transcript.

en_definition: the official act of granting an academic degree to a graduate
example1: The conferral of degree happens at the graduation ceremony.
example1_ru: Присвоение степени происходит на церемонии вручения дипломов.
example2: Formal conferral of degree requires payment of all outstanding fees.
